edges.description: An extra X chromosome elevates the dosage of X-linked escape genes, perturbing chromatin and transcription beyond what is observed in 46,XY.
edges.target: X-chromosome Inactivation Escape / Gene Dosage Imbalance

edges.description: 47,XXY karyotype drives progressive seminiferous-tubule, Sertoli/Leydig, and microvascular dysfunction in the testis.
Sertoli-cell and seminiferous tubule dysfunction drives germ-cell apoptosis and progressive depletion through puberty.
Leydig-cell dysfunction reduces testosterone production while elevated LH/FSH reflect loss of negative feedback, producing primary hypogonadism.

nodes.description: Leydig cell insufficiency and impaired testosterone export result in elevated LH/FSH and low systemic testosterone despite potentially elevated intratesticular testosterone.
Typically results in a 47,XXY karyotype, leading to a range of physical, developmental, and reproductive issues.

evidence.reference_title: Klinefelter syndrome and other sex chromosomal aneuploidies.
evidence.snippet: The term Klinefelter syndrome (KS) describes a group of chromosomal disorder in which there is at least one extra X chromosome to a normal male karyotype, 46,XY. XXY aneuploidy is the most common disorder of sex chromosomes in humans, with prevalence of one in 500 males.

evidence.reference_title: Epigenetics and genomics in Klinefelter syndrome.
evidence.snippet: Since the first description of Klinefelter syndrome (KS) was published in 1942...large inter-individual variability in the phenotypic presentation has been demonstrated... Evidence from the existing literature of KS indicates that not just one single genetic mechanism can explain the phenotype and the variable expressivity.

evidence.reference_title: Neuropsychology and socioeconomic aspects of Klinefelter syndrome: new developments.
evidence.snippet: Klinefelter syndrome is the most common sex-chromosome disorder in humans, affecting one in 660 men. The key findings in Klinefelter syndrome are small testes, hypergonadotropic hypogonadism and cognitive impairment.

nodes.description: Accelerated germ cell apoptosis beginning around puberty leads to spermatogenic failure and azoospermia in adulthood.
Immature Sertoli and Leydig cells, proinflammatory macrophage enrichment, extracellular matrix remodeling and microvascular dysfunction impair spermatogenesis and testosterone export.

In 47,XXY, the inactive X chromosome (Xi) does not fully silence expression of a subset of X-linked escape genes (notably DDX3X, USP9X, KDM6A, and other dosage-sensitive loci). The resulting bi-allelic expression of these genes elevates effective gene dosage above the typical male single-X level, contributing to neurodevelopmental, cognitive, and metabolic phenotypes independent of androgen deficiency. The Xi also exerts trans regulatory effects on active-X and autosomal gene expression, broadening the molecular impact of sex-chromosome aneuploidy beyond classic escape.
nodes.id: X-chromosome Inactivation Escape / Gene Dosage Imbalance

evidence.reference_title: X chromosome dosage and the genetic impact across human tissues
evidence.snippet: Fourteen X chromosomal genes were downregulated in 45,X and upregulated in 47,XXY, respectively, in all three tissues (AKAP17A,CD99,DHRSX,EIF2S3,GTPBP6,JPX,KDM6A,PP2R3B,PUDP,SLC25A6,TSIX,XIST,ZBED1,ZFX).

evidence.reference_title: Epigenetics and genomics in Klinefelter syndrome.
evidence.snippet: Evidence from the existing literature of KS indicates that not just one single genetic mechanism can explain the phenotype and the variable expressivity.
